specific_location: Far east end of Adobe Creek Reservoir, on east side of trees bordering water
description: At about 4:30 PM a nighthawk flushed from the ground where some short trees met barren desert-like ground. It flew towards but arcing around us, then past us, and around the far edge of the trees. Initially it was 100 or so feet in front of us, and when it disappeared it was 200 or so feet past us. At its closest approach it was 20 feet away at eye level. It never flew higher than 10 or so feet above the ground and never vocalized. The nighthawk seemed immediately odd as it flew differently from typical Common Nighthawks. The flight was more direct and the wings did not appear to rise as high above the body as in Common Nighthawk. As it came more into side view, the rounded wingtips were quite evident; furthermore, the white slash on the primaries appeared to be 75-80% towards tip of wing from bend in wing (To me, on CONI, the slash appears about 50% of the way between bend and tip of wing). I was unable to ascertain the shape of the white primary bar, but the distal wing did have some warm brown spotting, in contrast to the typical solid dark appearance of the outer wing on a CONI. The body and wings appeared warm brown, at least below, without obvious gray hues. The throat was white. We were unable to relocate the bird.Behavior: See DescriptionCall: Not heardPlumage: adult
